headline: EU top court to rule on record 4.1 bn euro Google fine
summary: The EU's top court will decide Thursday whether to uphold a record 4.1 billion euro ($4.7 billion) fine the bloc slapped on Google for anti-competitive practices related to its Android operating system.The commission, the EU's antitrust regulator, had accused Google of abusing the popularity of its Android operating system to restrict competition.

content.title: EU top court to rule on record 4.1 bn euro Google fine
content.summary: The EU's top court will decide Thursday whether to uphold a record 4.1 billion euro ($4.7 billion) fine the bloc slapped on Google for anti-competitive practices related to its Android operating system.The commission, the EU's antitrust regulator, had accused Google of abusing the popularity of its Android operating system to restrict competition.

thumbnail.caption: Google has been trying to overturn a record EU antitrust penalty before the European Court of Justice (Nicolas TUCAT)

headline: Google loses fight against record €4.1 billion EU antitrust fine
summary: Alphabet's Google on Thursday lost its fight against a record fine imposed by ‌EU antitrust regulators eight years ago for using its Android mobile operating ‌system to block rivals, a court ruling likely to boost Europe's crackdown on Big Tech.  The European ​Commission had originally handed out a €4.34 billion fine to Google in 2018 for its agreements which forced phone manufacturers to pre-install Google Search, the Chrome browser and the Google Play app store on their Android devices and prevented them from using rival ‌Android systems.  Google then appealed ⁠to the Luxembourg-based Court of Justice of the European Union, Europe's highest.

content.title: Google loses fight against record €4.1 billion EU antitrust fine
content.summary: Alphabet's Google on Thursday lost its fight against a record fine imposed by ‌EU antitrust regulators eight years ago for using its Android mobile operating ‌system to block rivals, a court ruling likely to boost Europe's crackdown on Big Tech.  The European ​Commission had originally handed out a €4.34 billion fine to Google in 2018 for its agreements which forced phone manufacturers to pre-install Google Search, the Chrome browser and the Google Play app store on their Android devices and prevented them from using rival ‌Android systems.  Google then appealed ⁠to the Luxembourg-based Court of Justice of the European Union, Europe's highest.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: A Google logo is seen at a company research facility in Mountain View, California
